text: The two situations are not remotely comparable. For one thing, the proposed Liberal-NDP coalition of 2008 still needed the votes of the Bloc Quebecois to sustain it. It was the reluctance of many Liberals to lead a government beholden to the Bloc that led the new leader, Michael Ignatieff, to renege on the deal his predecessor Stephane Dion had signed with Jack Layton. As well, because the Conservatives had held office for less than three years, their supporters could claim, with some justification, that they deserved a fair go.\n\nIn the current case, the NDP and the Greens have a slim but decisive majority in the Legislature. Neither party has reason to back out after being in opposition for so long. Meanwhile, after 16 years in power, the Liberals' time is up. They know better than to let Clark press for fresh elections--a move that would only provoke a sharp backlash from the public. Cooler heads will prevail; others, including Clark's, will likely roll.

text:
Governments will always be predisposed towards tax-and-spend.
It takes either an extreme financial/fiscal crisis, or a government with a strong sense of ideology/discipline to prod governments towards the straight and narrow path of fiscal prudence. Not to mention how hard it is to root out entrenched interests (public employee payrolls are a bulging low-hanging fruit, but good luck to those who try to prune it).

Even our last Conservative government (despite a good start) was not immune.
And the Liberal governments of the 90s adopted fiscal conservatism out of necessity.

For those of us who question the efficacy of government efforts/spending to spur the economy, or the benefits of heavy-handed government intervention into individuals' lives and the market, the only logical solution is to put more power into the hands of the individual citizen by shrinking government. Good luck with that.

 == Development (cheeky meanings of SECAM) == 

 
 NTSC was considered undesirable in Europe because of its tint problem requiring an additional control, which SECAM and PAL solved. The joke was that ""SECAM"" stood for «System Essentially Contrary to the American Method» versus NTSC «Never Twice the Same Color» whilst «Peace At Last» could only be obtained through the PAL system 

 I had heard (possibly in the early 1980s) - when I hung out with video people, who were possibly early Digital TV advocates - that ""SECAM"" stood for Silly Europeans Continuing American mistakes - this phrase seemed to be in common use in New England at the time, as I recall. 

 This seems to ignore the fact that the 'color/tint' problem was solved by PAL and SECAM."

text:
Yes... and?

That is why in my post I wrote "as such they have protection at international law that we have enshrined in our domestic legislation." That domestic legislation is enforceable.

As far as reform, the legislation and processes we now have are in place because in 1985 the Supreme Court of Canada determined that the lack of an oral hearing for a refugee claimant before an independent tribunal was unconstitutional. 

See Singh v Canada (Minister of Employment and Immigration), [1985] 1 S.C.R. 177

Singh determined that any person in Canada even without legal status was protected by the Charter. That decision was based on section 7 rights to security of person and fundamental justice. The SCOC ruled the government's claim that giving hearing to refugees would be burdensome was too utilitarian a concern, and that administrative convenience would rarely be sufficiently compelling to justify infringing a Charter right.

So good luck getting changes past the courts.

LISTEN EVERYBODY AND LEARN: THIS INFORMATION JUST MIGHT SAVE A LIFE:
- When a child eats a protein for the very first time, the body's digestive system 'filters' this protein so that it is not such a huge shock to the system. If small quantities are eaten over time the body slowly becomes 'used' to it and no allergies are developed. 
 On the other hand, if you take a child who has never had their body exposed to that protein, and you inject that protein directly into their bloodstream, the child develops an INSTANT allergy to that protein.
 Guess what is in most of today's commercial vaccines? PEANUT PROTEINS.
